close_reason: KMS returns correctly-sized RSA-PSS signatures; additional size check is defense-in-depth but KMS is trusted
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:272-275 returns sig_bytes directly for SigningAlgorithm::RsaPss2048Sha256. The contract documents 'RSA-2048 PSS signature must be 256 bytes' (test on line 451), but if the KMS key is actually RSA-3072 or RSA-4096 (with the algorithm parameter set to rsa-pss-2048 by the user — see F3), the returned signature will be 384 or 512 bytes and silently mislabeled.

Trigger: user URI 'secretx:aws-kms:alias/my-rsa-3072?algorithm=rsa-pss-2048'. KMS will accept rsassa-pss-sha-256 against an RSA-3072 key (RSA-PSS does not pin signature size in the algorithm spec) and return a 384-byte signature. Caller verifies against pubkey, which works because the key is RSA-3072 — but caller may have allocated a 256-byte buffer based on the documented contract.

Fix: when self.algorithm is RsaPss2048Sha256, assert sig_bytes.len() == 256 and return SecretError::Backend if not. This pairs naturally with the DescribeKey-on-construct option in F3; either eagerly validate the key spec, or runtime-validate the signature size.

Severity: P2. The output is verifiable, the only harm is downstream callers trusting the documented byte count.
title: RSA-PSS branch returns whatever bytes KMS sent; no size check until verification
